questions.question: List and explain the three types of comments in Java with examples.
keyPoints: Single-line comments (//): Used for short explanations, example: // This is a single-line comment
Multi-line comments (/* */): Used for longer explanations spanning multiple lines
Documentation comments (/** */): Used for generating API documentation with Javadoc

questions.question: What is Javadoc? Explain its purpose and mention any four standard Javadoc tags.
keyPoints: Javadoc is a tool that generates HTML documentation from Java source code

questions.question: Explain why comments are important in programming despite being non-executable.
keyPoints: Help in code maintenance and debugging
Make code easier to understand after long time

questions.question: Write a Java method with appropriate Javadoc comments including @param, @return, and @throws tags.
Can multi-line comments be nested in Java? Justify your answer with an example.
keyPoints: No, Java does not support nested comments
Compiler treats the first */ as end of comment

questions.question: Differentiate between single-line and multi-line comments in Java with syntax and examples.
keyPoints: Single-line (//): Comments one line only, syntax: // comment text

Multi-line (/* */): Comments multiple lines, syntax: /* comment
line 2 */
Scope difference: // affects only current line

questions.question: What are the best practices for writing effective comments in Java?
keyPoints: Keep comments updated with code changes
Use Javadoc for public APIs and classes
Use consistent style throughout the codebase

questions.question: Explain in detail the three types of comments in Java with syntax, examples, and their purposes. Also demonstrate how Javadoc comments are used to generate documentation.
keyPoints: 1. Single-line comments: // syntax, usage for brief explanations
2. Multi-line comments: /* */ syntax, usage for longer explanations
3. Documentation comments: /** */ syntax, Javadoc tool usage
5. Command: javadoc to generate HTML docs

questions.question: Write a complete Java class with proper Javadoc documentation for the class and its methods. Include appropriate Javadoc tags and explain how the Javadoc tool processes these comments.
Discuss the importance of comments in software development. What are the best practices for commenting? How does Javadoc contribute to Java documentation?
keyPoints: Best practices: meaningful comments, update with code, avoid redundancy

questions.question: Explain what happens during compilation when the Java compiler encounters comments. Also discuss nested comments and why they are not supported in Java.
keyPoints: Comments are not part of bytecode
First */ ends comment, remaining text causes syntax error
